query: Let's collect some data on beetle body sizes. I want to focus on Coleoptera.
Run a session and continue collecting karyotype data.
How many species do we have so far? Can we run QC on the data?
I dropped some PDFs in the pdfs/ folder. Can you extract the morphometric data from them?
Trawl for data on avian body mass from the literature.
Plan the campaign — what families still need coverage?
Audit the database and re-check the low-confidence records.
I want to build a trait database of mating systems across all bird families. Can you mine the literature for me?
continue collecting
I have about 40 papers here that report chromosome numbers for grasshoppers. Can you process them and add the records to my database?
What's known about sex chromosome evolution in beetles?
Summarize this paper for me.
Collect some thoughts on this paper and give me feedback.
Make me a chart showing the distribution of body sizes in my CSV.
Help me write the methods section for my paper.
I need to find papers about how CRISPR has been used in beetle research — what are the open questions and untested hypotheses?
Can you read this PDF and pull out the key findings? It's a review of lepidopteran karyotype diversity.
I have a spreadsheet with chromosome counts for 500 species. Can you clean it up, fix the formatting, and add a column for family?
What gaps exist in our understanding of insect chromosome evolution? Draft an introduction paragraph for my manuscript.
Run a statistical analysis on results.csv — I want to know if chromosome number correlates with body size across families.
